x-enumeration-literals.name: Demographic Model
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Analyze customer behavior based on demographic factors such as age, gender, income, education, and occupation.
Example: Segmenting customers by age group to tailor marketing strategies.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Psychographic Model
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Focus on psychological attributes, including values, attitudes, interests, and lifestyles.
Example: Creating buyer personas based on lifestyle choices and interests.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Geographic Model
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Look at customer behavior based on geographic location, considering factors like regional preferences and climate.
Example: Targeting advertisements based on location-specific needs.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Socioeconomic Model
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Combine social and economic factors to understand consumer behavior
Example: Analyzing how economic status influences buying decisions

x-enumeration-literals.name: Situational Model
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Consider the context or situation in which the consumer is making a decision, including time of day, season, and situational needs.
Example: Promoting holiday-related products during festive seasons.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Cultural Model
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Focus on the influence of culture, traditions, and social norms on consumer behavior.
Example: Adapting marketing messages to align with cultural values and practices.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Usage-Based Model
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Segment customers based on how they use a product or service, including frequency and intensity of use.
Example: Differentiating between heavy, moderate, and light users of a product.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Benefit-Sought Model
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Classify customers according to the benefits they seek from a product or service.
Example: Marketing products based on specific benefits like convenience, cost-saving, or luxury.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Hybrid Model
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Combine elements from various models to provide a comprehensive view of customer behavior.
Example: Using demographic, psychographic, and behavioral data together for more precise targeting
